description: nginx web-server / reverse-proxy config invariants. Catches weak TLS protocol versions, version-banner disclosure, directory listing, and proxy upstream TLS verification turned off. Conservative and directive-anchored so it does not fire on prose or unrelated config keys. Apply to any repo that ships nginx config.

rules.//: ssl_protocols including SSLv2/SSLv3 (POODLE) or TLSv1.0/TLSv1.1 (deprecated by RFC 8996, BEAST) negotiates protocols with known breaks. The alternation uses TLSv1(?!\.\d) so a bare `TLSv1` (= TLS 1.0) matches but `TLSv1.2`/`TLSv1.3` do NOT — a plain \bTLSv1\b would false-positive because a word boundary sits between `TLSv1` and the following dot. TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1 are listed explicitly. [^;\n]* keeps the scan inside the single directive value. HARD: there is no safe reason to enable these in new config.
rules.message: nginx ssl_protocols enables a weak protocol (SSLv2/SSLv3/TLSv1.0/TLSv1.1) — these have known breaks (POODLE, BEAST) and are deprecated by RFC 8996. Pin `ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;` (TLS 1.3 alone where the client base allows).

rules.//: server_tokens on emits the exact nginx version in the Server header and error pages, handing an attacker a free version-to-CVE lookup. The default is on, so an explicit `on` is a deliberate disclosure choice worth flagging. WARN because banner hiding is defense-in-depth, not a vulnerability by itself. `\bon\b` will not match `off`.
rules.message: nginx server_tokens on exposes the exact nginx version (Server header, error pages) — set `server_tokens off;` to avoid handing out a version-to-CVE lookup.

rules.//: autoindex on turns a directory with no index file into a browsable file listing, frequently leaking backups, .env files, and source. WARN because a deliberately public file index is occasionally intended. `\bon\b` will not match `off`.
rules.message: nginx autoindex on enables directory listing — a directory without an index file becomes browsable, often leaking backups/.env/source. Set `autoindex off;` unless a public file index is genuinely intended.

rules.//: proxy_ssl_verify off disables certificate validation on the upstream (origin) TLS connection, so nginx accepts any cert from the backend — a MITM on the proxy-to-origin hop. WARN because some internal upstreams use a private CA that is configured separately; the right fix is proxy_ssl_trusted_certificate, not turning verification off. `\boff\b` will not match `on`.
rules.message: nginx proxy_ssl_verify off disables upstream TLS certificate validation (MITM on the proxy-to-origin hop). Set `proxy_ssl_verify on;` and point `proxy_ssl_trusted_certificate` at the upstream CA.
